---
title: "Model Speed Benchmark: Hierarchical vs Single F1 Model"
jupyter: python3
---

## Setup: CPU Configuration & Imports

```{python}
# === CPU SETUP ===
import tensorflow as tf
import os

cpu_count = os.cpu_count()
print(f"CPU cores detected: {cpu_count}")

tf.config.threading.set_intra_op_parallelism_threads(cpu_count)
tf.config.threading.set_inter_op_parallelism_threads(cpu_count)

os.environ['TF_ENABLE_ONEDNN_OPTS'] = '1'
os.environ['KMP_BLOCKTIME'] = '0'
os.environ['KMP_SETTINGS'] = '1'
os.environ['OMP_NUM_THREADS'] = str(cpu_count)

print("CPU threading and oneDNN optimizations configured.")
```

```{python}
import os
import time
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import tensorflow as tf
from tensorflow.keras.preprocessing.image import ImageDataGenerator, load_img, img_to_array
import cv2
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.metrics import accuracy_score, classification_report

# === PATHS ===
DATA_DIR = os.path.join(os.path.abspath('.'), 'roadsigns_data')
model_save_dir = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'saved_models')
training_dir = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'training')
holdout_dir = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'holdout')
mini_holdout_dir = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'mini_holdout')
mini_holdout_answers = os.path.join(DATA_DIR, 'mini_holdout_answers.csv')

image_size = (100, 100)
```

## Preprocessing (shared by both models)

```{python}
def apply_clahe_color(image):
    """Optimized CLAHE for small 100x100 images."""
    img_uint8 = image.astype(np.uint8)
    lab = cv2.cvtColor(img_uint8, cv2.COLOR_RGB2LAB)
    l_channel, a_channel, b_channel = cv2.split(lab)
    clahe = cv2.createCLAHE(clipLimit=1.1, tileGridSize=(4, 4))
    cl = clahe.apply(l_channel)
    merged_lab = cv2.merge((cl, a_channel, b_channel))
    enhanced_rgb = cv2.cvtColor(merged_lab, cv2.COLOR_LAB2RGB)
    return enhanced_rgb.astype(np.float32)


def val_preprocessing(image):
    """Applied to VALIDATION and TEST data: CLAHE enhancement ONLY"""
    return apply_clahe_color(image)
```

## Load Models

```{python}
# === LOAD SINGLE F1 MODEL ===
print("Loading single F1 model...")
model_single = tf.keras.models.load_model(
    os.path.join(model_save_dir, 'best_road_signs_f1_model.keras'),
    compile=False
)
print(f"Single model input shape: {model_single.input_shape}")
print(f"Single model output classes: {model_single.output_shape}")
```

```{python}
# === LOAD HIERARCHICAL MODELS ===
print("Loading hierarchical models...")
model_router = tf.keras.models.load_model(
    os.path.join(model_save_dir, 'router_model.keras'), compile=False)
model_speed  = tf.keras.models.load_model(
    os.path.join(model_save_dir, 'speed_model.keras'), compile=False)
model_arrow  = tf.keras.models.load_model(
    os.path.join(model_save_dir, 'arrow_model.keras'), compile=False)
model_hazard = tf.keras.models.load_model(
    os.path.join(model_save_dir, 'hazard_model.keras'), compile=False)
print("All 4 hierarchical models loaded.")
```

## Label Definitions & Routing Logic

```{python}
# === LABEL LISTS ===
target_names = ['Speed_20', 'Speed_30', 'Speed_50', 'Speed_60', 'Speed_70',
               'Speed_80','Speed_Limit_Ends', 'Speed_100', 'Speed_120', 'Overtaking_Prohibited',
               'Overtaking_Prohibited_Trucks', 'Priority', 'Priority_Road_Ahead', 'Yield', 'STOP',
               'Entry_Forbidden', 'Trucks_Forbidden', 'No_Entry(one-way traffic)', 'General Danger(!)', 'Left_Curve_Ahead',
               'Right_Curve_Ahead', 'Double_Curve', 'Poor_Surface_Ahead', 'Slippery_Surface_Ahead', 'Road_Narrows_On_Right',
               'Roadwork_Ahead', 'Traffic_Light_Ahead', 'Warning_Pedestrians', 'Warning_Children', 'Warning_Bikes',
               'Ice_Snow', 'Deer_Crossing', 'End_Previous_Limitation', 'Turning_Right_Compulsory', 'Turning_Left_Compulsory',
               'Ahead_Only', 'Straight_Or_Right_Mandatory', 'Straight_Or_Left_Mandatory', 'Passing_Right_Compulsory', 'Passing_Left_Compulsory',
               'Roundabout', 'End_Overtaking_Prohibition', 'End_Overtaking_Prohibition_Trucks']

router_labels = ['Family_Speed', 'Family_Arrow', 'Family_Hazard', 'STOP', 'Yield', 'Priority', 'No_Entry']

speed_labels = [
    'Speed_20', 'Speed_30', 'Speed_50', 'Speed_60', 'Speed_70', 
    'Speed_80', 'Speed_Limit_Ends', 'Speed_100', 'Speed_120', 
    'Overtaking_Prohibited', 'Overtaking_Prohibited_Trucks', 
    'Entry_Forbidden', 'Trucks_Forbidden', 'End_Previous_Limitation',
    'End_Overtaking_Prohibition', 'End_Overtaking_Prohibition_Trucks'
]

hazard_labels = [
    'Priority', 'General Danger(!)', 'Left_Curve_Ahead',
    'Right_Curve_Ahead', 'Double_Curve', 'Poor_Surface_Ahead', 
    'Slippery_Surface_Ahead', 'Road_Narrows_On_Right', 'Roadwork_Ahead', 
    'Traffic_Light_Ahead', 'Warning_Pedestrians', 'Warning_Children', 
    'Warning_Bikes', 'Ice_Snow', 'Deer_Crossing'
]

arrow_labels = [
    'Turning_Right_Compulsory', 'Turning_Left_Compulsory', 'Ahead_Only', 
    'Straight_Or_Right_Mandatory', 'Straight_Or_Left_Mandatory', 
    'Passing_Right_Compulsory', 'Passing_Left_Compulsory', 'Roundabout'
]
```

```{python}
# === HIERARCHICAL ROUTING FUNCTION ===
def hierarchical_inference_batched(image_batch):
    """Run hierarchical inference on a full batch of images at once."""
    router_preds = model_router.predict(image_batch, verbose=0)
    predicted_families = np.argmax(router_preds, axis=1)

    final_preds = np.zeros(len(image_batch), dtype=int)

    family_to_model = {
        'Family_Speed': (model_speed, speed_labels),
        'Family_Arrow': (model_arrow, arrow_labels),
        'Family_Hazard': (model_hazard, hazard_labels),
    }

    family_name_to_idx = {name: i for i, name in enumerate(router_labels)}

    for family_name, (expert_model, expert_labels) in family_to_model.items():
        family_idx = family_name_to_idx[family_name]
        mask = predicted_families == family_idx
        if not np.any(mask):
            continue
        expert_images = image_batch[mask]
        expert_preds = expert_model.predict(expert_images, verbose=0)
        expert_class_indices = np.argmax(expert_preds, axis=1)
        for i, idx in enumerate(np.where(mask)[0]):
            label_name = expert_labels[expert_class_indices[i]]
            final_preds[idx] = target_names.index(label_name)

    for family_name in ['STOP', 'Yield', 'No_Entry', 'Priority']:
        family_idx = family_name_to_idx[family_name]
        mask = predicted_families == family_idx
        if not np.any(mask):
            continue
        if family_name == 'No_Entry':
            final_preds[mask] = target_names.index('No_Entry(one-way traffic)')
        elif family_name == 'Priority':
            final_preds[mask] = target_names.index('Priority_Road_Ahead')
        else:
            final_preds[mask] = target_names.index(family_name)

    return final_preds
```

## Prepare Test Data

```{python}
# === LOAD MINI HOLDOUT IMAGES AS A SINGLE BENCHMARK SET ===
# We use the mini_holdout set so we have ground truth AND real images.
# This gives us a realistic mix of all 43 classes.

test_datagen = ImageDataGenerator(rescale=1./255, preprocessing_function=val_preprocessing)
test_generator = test_datagen.flow_from_directory(
    DATA_DIR,
    classes=['mini_holdout'],
    target_size=image_size,
    batch_size=10000,   # grab all at once
    class_mode='sparse',
    shuffle=False
)

# Pull all images into memory as a single numpy array
all_images, _ = test_generator[0]
answers_df = pd.read_csv(mini_holdout_answers)
y_true = answers_df['ClassId'].values

print(f"Loaded {len(all_images)} test images, shape: {all_images.shape}")
print(f"Ground truth labels: {len(y_true)}")
```

## Warm-up Run (exclude TF initialization overhead)

```{python}
# === WARM-UP ===
# Run a small batch through each model once to warm up TF's graph/XLA,
# so subsequent timings reflect steady-state performance.
print("Warming up models (excluded from timings)...")
_ = model_single.predict(all_images[:10], verbose=0)
_ = hierarchical_inference_batched(all_images[:10])
print("Warm-up complete.\n")
```

## Benchmark: Single Image Inference

```{python}
# ============================================================
# BENCHMARK 1: SINGLE IMAGE (n=1)
# ============================================================
print("=" * 60)
print("BENCHMARK 1 — Single Image Inference (n=1)")
print("=" * 60)

single_img = all_images[:1:2]  # shape (1, 100, 100, 3)

# --- Single F1 model ---
n_trials = 20
times_single_1 = []
for _ in range(n_trials):
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    _ = model_single.predict(single_img, verbose=0)
    t1 = time.perf_counter()
    times_single_1.append(t1 - t0)

# --- Hierarchical model ---
times_hier_1 = []
for _ in range(n_trials):
    t0 = time.perf_counter()
    _ = hierarchical_inference_batched(single_img)
    t1 = time.perf_counter()
    times_hier_1.append(t1 - t0)

print(f"\n{'Model':<25} {'Mean (ms)':<12} {'Std (ms)':<12} {'Min (ms)':<12} {'Median (ms)':<12}")
print("-" * 73)
for name, times in [("Single F1", times_single_1), ("Hierarchical", times_hier_1)]:
    t = np.array(times) * 1000
    print(f"{name:<25} {t.mean():<12.2f} {t.std():<12.2f} {t.min():<12.2f} {np.median(t):<12.2f}")

speedup_1 = np.median(times_hier_1) / np.median(times_single_1)
print(f"\nHierarchical / Single ratio: {speedup_1:.2f}x")
```

## Benchmark: Batch Inference Across Multiple Sizes

```{python}
# ============================================================
# BENCHMARK 2: BATCH INFERENCE ACROSS MULTIPLE SIZES
# ============================================================
print("\n" + "=" * 60)
print("BENCHMARK 2 — Batch Inference (multiple sizes)")
print("=" * 60)

batch_sizes = [1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024]
# Cap at available images
batch_sizes = [b for b in batch_sizes if b <= len(all_images)]

n_repeats = 15  # number of repeats per batch size for averaging

results = []

for bs in batch_sizes:
    batch = all_images[:bs]

    # --- Single F1 ---
    times_s = []
    for _ in range(n_repeats):
        t0 = time.perf_counter()
        _ = model_single.predict(batch, verbose=0)
        t0_end = time.perf_counter()
        times_s.append(t0_end - t0)

    # --- Hierarchical ---
    times_h = []
    for _ in range(n_repeats):
        t0 = time.perf_counter()
        _ = hierarchical_inference_batched(batch)
        t0_end = time.perf_counter()
        times_h.append(t0_end - t0)

    med_s = np.median(times_s) * 1000
    med_h = np.median(times_h) * 1000
    per_img_s = med_s / bs
    per_img_h = med_h / bs

    results.append({
        'batch_size': bs,
        'single_total_ms': med_s,
        'hier_total_ms': med_h,
        'single_per_img_ms': per_img_s,
        'hier_per_img_ms': per_img_h,
        'ratio_total': med_h / med_s if med_s > 0 else float('inf'),
        'ratio_per_img': per_img_h / per_img_s if per_img_s > 0 else float('inf'),
    })

results_df = pd.DataFrame(results)

print(f"\n{'Batch':>6} | {'Single Total':>13} | {'Hier Total':>11} | "
      f"{'Single/img':>11} | {'Hier/img':>9} | {'Ratio':>7} | {'Ratio/img':>10}")
print("-" * 90)
for _, r in results_df.iterrows():
    print(f"{int(r['batch_size']):>6} | "
          f"{r['single_total_ms']:>10.2f} ms | "
          f"{r['hier_total_ms']:>8.2f} ms | "
          f"{r['single_per_img_ms']:>8.4f} ms | "
          f"{r['hier_per_img_ms']:>6.4f} ms | "
          f"{r['ratio_total']:>5.2f}x | "
          f"{r['ratio_per_img']:>7.2f}x")
```

## Visualization: Total Time vs Batch Size

```{python}
# === PLOT: Total inference time vs batch size ===
fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(14, 6))

# Plot 1: Total time
ax = axes[0]
ax.plot(results_df['batch_size'], results_df['single_total_ms'], 'o-', label='Single F1', linewidth=2)
ax.plot(results_df['batch_size'], results_df['hier_total_ms'], 's-', label='Hierarchical', linewidth=2)
ax.set_xlabel('Batch Size')
ax.set_ylabel('Total Time (ms)')
ax.set_title('Total Inference Time vs Batch Size')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
ax.set_xscale('log', base=2)
y_max = ax.get_ylim()[1]
ax.set_ylim(0, y_max)
ax.set_yticks(np.arange(0, y_max + 250, 250))


# Plot 2: Per-image time
ax = axes[1]
ax.plot(results_df['batch_size'], results_df['single_per_img_ms'], 'o-', label='Single F1', linewidth=2)
ax.plot(results_df['batch_size'], results_df['hier_per_img_ms'], 's-', label='Hierarchical', linewidth=2)
ax.set_xlabel('Batch Size')
ax.set_ylabel('Per-Image Time (ms)')
ax.set_title('Per-Image Inference Time vs Batch Size')
ax.legend()
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
ax.set_xscale('log', base=2)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Visualization: Side-by-Side Bar Chart

```{python}
# === PLOT: Side-by-side grouped bar chart ===
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))

x = np.arange(len(results_df))
width = 0.35

bars1 = ax.bar(x - width/2, results_df['single_total_ms'], width, label='Single F1', color='#2196F3', alpha=0.85)
bars2 = ax.bar(x + width/2, results_df['hier_total_ms'], width, label='Hierarchical', color='#FF9800', alpha=0.85)

ax.set_xlabel('Batch Size')
ax.set_ylabel('Median Total Time (ms)')
ax.set_title('Model Inference Speed: Hierarchical vs Single F1')
ax.set_xticks(x)
ax.set_xticklabels(results_df['batch_size'].astype(int))
ax.legend()
ax.grid(True, alpha=0.3, axis='y')

# Add value labels on bars
for bar in bars1:
    ax.annotate(f'{bar.get_height():.1f}',
                xy=(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2, bar.get_height()),
                xytext=(0, 3), textcoords="offset points",
                ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=8)
for bar in bars2:
    ax.annotate(f'{bar.get_height():.1f}',
                xy=(bar.get_x() + bar.get_width() / 2, bar.get_height()),
                xytext=(0, 3), textcoords="offset points",
                ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=8)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

## Accuracy Comparison (Sanity Check)

```{python}
# ============================================================
# ACCURACY COMPARISON — make sure both models agree on quality
# ============================================================
print("=" * 60)
print("ACCURACY COMPARISON (mini holdout)")
print("=" * 60)

# Single F1 predictions
single_preds = np.argmax(model_single.predict(all_images, verbose=0), axis=1)
# Hierarchical predictions
hier_preds = hierarchical_inference_batched(all_images)

acc_single = accuracy_score(y_true, single_preds)
acc_hier = accuracy_score(y_true, hier_preds)

print(f"\nSingle F1 model accuracy:    {acc_single * 100:.2f}%")
print(f"Hierarchical model accuracy: {acc_hier * 100:.2f}%")
print(f"Accuracy difference:         {(acc_hier - acc_single) * 100:+.2f}%")

# Agreement between the two models
agreement = accuracy_score(single_preds, hier_preds)
print(f"Inter-model agreement:       {agreement * 100:.2f}%")
```

## Summary Table

```{python}
# ============================================================
# FINAL SUMMARY
# ============================================================
print("\n" + "=" * 70)
print("FINAL SUMMARY")
print("=" * 70)

print(f"\nAccuracy:")
print(f"  Single F1:    {acc_single*100:.2f}%")
print(f"  Hierarchical: {acc_hier*100:.2f}%")

print(f"\nSpeed (per-image, from largest batch):")
largest = results_df.iloc[-1]
print(f"  Single F1:    {largest['single_per_img_ms']:.4f} ms/image  ({1000/largest['single_per_img_ms']:.0f} img/s)")
print(f"  Hierarchical: {largest['hier_per_img_ms']:.4f} ms/image  ({1000/largest['hier_per_img_ms']:.0f} img/s)")
print(f"  Overhead:     {largest['ratio_per_img']:.2f}x slower per image")

print(f"\nSpeed (single image, median of 20 trials):")
s1 = np.median(times_single_1) * 1000
h1 = np.median(times_hier_1) * 1000
print(f"  Single F1:    {s1:.2f} ms")
print(f"  Hierarchical: {h1:.2f} ms")
print(f"  Overhead:     {h1/s1:.2f}x slower")

print("\n" + "=" * 70)
```
